title: Borrow and redeem paths expose stale-liquidity reentrancy across markets
reason: Only the abstract base contracts are in scope here; no concrete `doTransferOut` implementation is present that shows an attacker-controlled callback path. Without a concrete market implementation that can reenter, the claim is too speculative to report.

title: Close Factor Not Validated on _setCloseFactor
reason: This is an admin-only parameter-setting path. Any harm depends on privileged misconfiguration by the trusted admin, which is not a standalone protocol vulnerability in this codebase.

title: Oracle Price Manipulation Risk
reason: The report describes the trusted admin's ability to replace the oracle. That is a governance/control assumption, not an exploitable bug by unprivileged actors.

title: No Timelock on Critical Admin Functions
reason: Absence of a timelock is a governance design choice rather than a code vulnerability. The candidate does not identify an unprivileged exploit path.

title: Liquidation Incentive Not Validated
reason: This is another admin-only misconfiguration scenario. Since the same admin can already make broader privileged changes, the missing bound is not a distinct reportable vulnerability here.

title: Interest Rate Model Can Be Changed Instantly
reason: Changing the interest rate model is an intended admin power guarded by `msg.sender == admin` plus interface checks. This is governance trust, not a protocol bug.

title: Comptroller Can Be Changed on CTokens
reason: `_setComptroller` is an explicit admin hook and verifies the new target implements the comptroller marker. The candidate is a generic privileged-upgrade concern, not a vulnerability.

title: Borrow Cap Guardian Can Disable Borrowing
reason: The claim is factually incorrect for this code: `_setMarketBorrowCaps` documents and implements `0` as unlimited borrowing, not disabled borrowing. More generally, borrow-cap setting is an intended privileged control.

title: COMP Accrual Fix Function Allows Arbitrary Adjustment
reason: The function is a one-off admin-only remediation hook and the candidate relies on trusted-admin abuse. The reportable issue in this area is instead that recorded `compReceivable` debt is never enforced.

title: Unitroller Delegatecall Reentrancy Risk
reason: This is the standard upgradeable proxy delegation pattern. The candidate does not show a distinct exploit beyond compromising the trusted implementation/admin path.

title: Market Deprecation Can Be Abused for Immediate Liquidation
reason: `isDeprecated` is an explicit admin-controlled deprecation mechanism. The ability to deprecate a market is an intended governance action, not an accidental bypass.

title: Pause Guardian Can Pause Critical Functions
reason: The pause guardian is an intentional emergency-control role documented in storage comments. This is a trust/governance feature, not a bug.
